_schema: MESSAGE_BOARD v2 — rebuilt 2026-03-16 session S178. Max 20 messages. Prune oldest on overflow.

messages.message: S178 SPRINT 2 (#65) — STRIPE BILLING IMPLEMENTATION COMPLETE ✅. All 4 tasks delivered: (1) Stripe billing endpoints — POST /api/billing/checkout, webhook handler, GET /api/billing/subscription, POST /api/billing/cancel (2) requireTier('PRO') wired to: POST /api/items/bulk (batch ops), GET /api/export/* (data exports), GET /api/insights/organizer (analytics) (3) /organizer/upgrade.tsx — tier comparison, monthly/annual toggle, 7-day trial callout, Stripe Checkout integration (4) /organizer/subscription.tsx — current plan display, billing interval, renewal date, cancel with confirmation modal. MODIFIED: index.ts (billing route + webhook raw body middleware), items.ts, export.ts, insights.ts, organizer/settings.tsx (added subscription tab). 11 files changed. Files ready for Patrick's .\push.ps1. Tests recommended: payment flow, tier gates, webhook idempotency.

ROADMAP TIER AUDIT COMPLETE — Session 176. File: /claude_docs/strategy/roadmap.md (v36). EDITS: (1) Inserted 'Shipped Features' section with 8 subsections (71+ features organized by category: organizer core/analytics/marketing/workflows, shopper discovery/engagement/gamification, platform/AI). All 31 undocumented features now documented + tiered. (2) Applied tier tags to 60+ pipeline features (Next Up, Phase 4, Phase 5, Vision). (3) Applied tier tags to 65+ deferred features. (4) Updated header timestamp (v35 → v36) with session 176 summary. METRICS: Shipped features counted = 71+. Tier tags applied to pipeline = 58. Tier tags applied to deferred = 65. TBD tier assignments = 1 (BUSINESS_PLAN.md rewrite). KEY DECISIONS LOCKED: Virtual Queue→[PRO], Social Templates→[SIMPLE], CSV exports→[SIMPLE], Flash Deals→[SIMPLE], OG Image Generator→[SIMPLE], Hunt Pass=[PAID_ADDON] $4.99/30d, Affiliate→[DEFER], AI Sale Planner Chat→[FREE], Brand Kit→[PRO]. Brand-spreading features [SIMPLE]: Social Templates, Share Card Factory, Cloudinary Watermark, CSV exports (tier-elevating marketing). No structural issues. Document ready for Patrick review.

DEVIL'S ADVOCATE — PRICING TIER CHALLENGE. Challenge to three debatable tier calls + Hunt Pass positioning. FINDINGS: (1) VIRTUAL QUEUE (currently ENTERPRISE): Risk — gate as SIMPLE and lose $29/mo if it drives adoption; gate as PRO and organizers never see it, killing demand. Failure: zero metrics, shelved as 'not valuable.' Before committing: 2-week beta with 5-10 SIMPLE organizers, ask 'in-app SMS queue: would you use it?' >60% = move to SIMPLE. Ask power users: manually manage lines now? >40% = pain point for SIMPLE. Check Etsy/eBay queue precedent. (2) AFFILIATE PROGRAM (currently ENTERPRISE): Risk — organizers recruit via regional networks, not links. Zero ROI for 5d build. Failure: Q4 2026 deferral buries it, dormant 6 months. Before committing: Interview 10 organizers on referral incentives. <5 'definitely' = defer. Check beta for organic referrals logged. Decide: growth hack (SIMPLE) or moat (ENTERPRISE)? Don't ship unless 3+ say 'I'd refer 5 friends if incentivized.' (3) COUPONS (PRO + 3-limit SIMPLE): Risk — SIMPLE looks broken if basic % coupons are table-stakes (check Etsy), but PRO feels too exclusive. 3-coupon quota fails if nobody hits limit. Failure: quota confusion, churn on limit. Before committing: 3-month test with 5 SIMPLE organizers — how many created coupons? <2 = remove from SIMPLE. Ask: marketing feature (PRO) or 'I need to run a sale' (SIMPLE)? Simplify: unlimited basic % (SIMPLE) OR remove entirely (PRO only). Don't create unused quotas. (4) HUNT PASS POSITIONING: Risk — standalone $4.99/30d + future Pro Shopper $9.99/mo = early payers feel punted on. Free Hunt Pass = lose revenue. Failure: Pro Shopper cannibalizes Hunt Pass, customer service nightmare. Before committing: Get Hunt Pass metrics NOW (weekly purchases, 30-day churn). Churn >50% = bundling saves it; <20% = standalone is fine. Survey 20-30 Hunt Pass users: upgrade to Pro Shopper? <30% yes = standalone works. If bundling: sunset clause for Hunt Pass → Pro Shopper migration, prorated. Option C (free) needs 2.5x Pro Shopper lift; justify with data. BOTTOM LINE: Get real beta data before committing. Hunt Pass is live revenue — don't move it on hopes. Test, measure, decide.

PRICING STRATEGY ARCHIVAL COMPLETE. Stale document archived (10% flat fee locked Session 106). Files changed: archive-index.json, roadmap.md, pricing-strategy.md (deleted). FLAG: BUSINESS_PLAN.md contains stale fee references (5–7% fees) — must update to reflect 10% flat.

GITHUB AUDIT TIER CLASSIFICATION COMPLETE. 36 undocumented features classified (SIMPLE/PRO/ENTERPRISE). FILE: claude_docs/operations/feature-tier-classification-2026-03-16.md. SUMMARY: 10 SIMPLE (checklist, waitlist, digest, inbox, reviews, disputes, invites, messaging), 5 PRO (pickup scheduling, flash deals, message templates, coupons, earnings PDF), 2 ENTERPRISE (virtual queue, affiliate). 11 FREE_SHOPPER (wishlists, searches, messaging, bounties, trending, activity feed, route planning, pages, profiles, leaderboards), 4 FREE_GAMIFICATION (points, streaks, treasure hunt, leaderboard), 1 STANDALONE_PAID (Hunt Pass $4.99/30d). 3 DECISION POINTS FLAGGED: (1) Virtual Queue — ENTERPRISE or PRO? (2) Affiliate — ENTERPRISE or defer? (3) Coupons — PRO or SIMPLE with limits? Pricing page copy production-ready.

FEATURE TIER MATRIX COMPLETE — ADR-065 Operationalization (8 sections, 550+ lines). All 71 features mapped SIMPLE/PRO/ENTERPRISE. 19 shipped (Rapidfire, AI tags, Health Score, Holds, POS, etc.) ALL SIMPLE. 13 queued PRO+. 39 future gated appropriately. SHOPPER tier deferral to S177+ pending 1k+ shoppers + 30% repeat. A-LA-CARTE candidates deferred post-beta. DECISIONS REQUIRED: (1) Tier mapping approval? (2) PREMIUM_SHOPPER defer? (3) A-LA-CARTE scope? (4) Tier upgrade UI? (5) Beta-to-paid timing? (6) Founding rate scope? Complements investor analysis + advisory board stress test.

PRICING ANALYSIS COMPLETE. RECOMMENDATIONS: PRO $29/month ($290/year, 20% discount). Breakeven at 1 sale/quarter. FOUNDING $99/year for 10 organizers (urgency + early revenue). A-LA-CARTE 6 features ($4.99–$9.99, Flip Report centerpiece). ENTERPRISE defer Q4 2026. SHOPPER 100% free forever. REVENUE: Y1 transaction fees 99%, subscriptions 1% (~$11.7k–$15.2k). Organizer acquisition velocity > pricing optimization. BETA TRANSITION: 60-day notice, feature lockdown day 1. DECISIONS: Approve $29 PRO? $99 Founding? A-LA-CARTE menu? 100% free shoppers? Enterprise defer?

ADVISORY BOARD PRICING STRESS TEST (ADR-065). Go-to-Market + Risk subcommittees. FINDINGS: Rename (SIMPLE→Essential, PRO→Pro, ENTERPRISE→Teams). Conversion 3-5% w/o sales team; friction needed (1-3 sales, 10-item bulk, no exports). PRO aha feature: Flip Report. Beta-to-paid: 6-month free goodwill. Founding $19/month 25 organizers preferred over $99/year. CONSENSUS: Announce Founding now, keep free during beta, launch paid Essential/Pro/Teams Q2 2026. CONTRARIAN: Invert fee model ($20/mo + 12-15% fee vs 10% + $30-60). RECOMMENDATIONS: (1) Audit SIMPLE features. (2) Write beta terms. (3) Tie PRO to ROI. (4) Vote on timing. Detailed analysis saved. FLAGGED: Feature audit, Founding scope, invert fee consideration, remove Enterprise, beta timing.
